title: RE-ROLL: B4 dial centre — broader turns, exactly four
prompt: Commercial food advertising photography, hyper-detailed and appetizing. Seamless saturated studio background in flat warm ghee gold #FFC933, with a subtle darker vignette at the bottom edge and slightly lighter glow upper-left. Punchy directional studio key light from upper left, crisp defined edges, crisp soft-edged drop shadow. Subject floating in mid-air unless stated. Small crumbs and particles suspended around the subject. No props, no surfaces, no hands, no text unless stated. Colors rich and saturated but food tones natural and true. Dead top-down view of a single bhajani chakli centred in frame like a vinyl record, perfectly circular silhouette. The chakli is ONE continuous rope of deeply ridged star-section extruded dough, as thick as a thumb, coiled into a tight FLAT Archimedean spiral of EXACTLY FOUR broad turns — count them: one, two, three, four — that touch each other with no gaps, winding continuously into the centre where the rope ends in a small tucked tail. The turns are broad and chunky so only four ridged rings are visible between rim and centre, matching the campaign hero chakli's coarse coil density. Even golden-brown fry colour, black and white sesame seeds embedded in the sharp ridges, faint satin oil sheen. Crisp soft-edged shadow directly beneath, a few crumbs and sesame seeds suspended just outside the rim. Not a rope pile, not a cinnamon roll, not jalebi, not concentric separate rings, no fifth or sixth turn, no gaps between turns.

title: RE-ROLL: B6 snap — spiral centre on one half only
prompt: Commercial food advertising photography, hyper-detailed and appetizing. Seamless saturated studio background in flat chili red #C8301B, with a subtle darker vignette at the bottom edge and slightly lighter glow upper-left. Punchy directional studio key light from upper left, crisp defined edges, crisp soft-edged drop shadow. Subject floating in mid-air unless stated. Small crumbs and particles suspended around the subject. No props, no surfaces, no hands, no text unless stated. Colors rich and saturated but food tones natural and true. ONE single bhajani chakli — a tight flat Archimedean spiral of four concentric touching turns, deeply ridged star-section extruded dough, golden-brown with black and white sesame seeds — snapped violently in half along one vertical break line, high-speed flash-freeze. The break passes through the whole disc, so the LEFT half keeps the spiral's coiled centre, and the RIGHT half is ONLY a stack of broken concentric arc segments with NO curl and NO centre of its own. The two halves are a few centimetres apart and still aligned like torn puzzle pieces of the same disc: every arc on the right half visually continues an arc on the left half. The matching rough fracture faces are angled slightly toward camera revealing coarse, airy, crisp fried interior crumb, and a violent blast of crumbs and sesame explodes outward from the break line. Not two whole chaklis, not two spirals, not mirror-image halves — one spiral disc broken into a centre half and an arc half.
